enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a video request from Brian Delacruz about social anxiety disorder. He attributes social anxiety to insecurity and people-pleasing rooted in upbringing, and advises going to public places not to perform but to observe others from a detached, confident 'king' mindset. He also ties the advice back to a prior request Brian made about a love triangle, suggesting Brian needs to stop seeking external validation entirely.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: rooted in insecurity from guilt-inducing parenting, not a clinical disorder per se
go to public places to watch people, not to impress them — divert attention outward
adopt a 'king' mindset — you're there for yourself, not to perform for others
practice holding eye contact with strangers to build a sense of owning the room
switching up your look can reset how people perceive and approach you
stop trying to validate yourself through others' reactions; just be present
steer clear — she's manipulative; find someone who accepts you as you are
